---
title: "Hypothesis v0.1 · Proposal Source"
subtitle: "Why the proposal is a hypothesis source, and which claims rest on it alone"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Evidence <span class="sep">›</span> Hypothesis v0.1 (Proposal Source)
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This page answers "what is asserted but not evidenced?"** — the claims that currently rest only on
the proposal.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-grade-d">Proposal = grade D</span>
<span class="pc-next">[See all claims](claims-confidence.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

The source proposal is a persuasive investor/operator document — an argument made *before* the
evidence is in. Treating it as truth would bake in untested assumptions. So it enters the lab as
**Hypothesis v0.1**: a structured set of falsifiable claims. Under the CIS evidence model,
proposal-sourced items carry **low quality and high bias risk**, so they can *raise* a claim but never
lift it above confidence grade **C** on their own.

```{python}
#| label: proposal
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))

from src.utils import load_sample
from IPython.display import HTML

register = load_sample("evidence_register.csv")
proposal = register[register["source_type"] == "proposal"].copy()

def metric(label, value, sub=""):
    sub_html = f'<div class="metric-sub">{sub}</div>' if sub else ""
    return (f'<div class="metric"><div class="metric-label">{label}</div>'
            f'<div class="metric-value">{value}</div>{sub_html}</div>')

share = 100 * len(proposal) / len(register)
HTML(
    '<div class="metric-row">'
    + metric("Proposal-sourced items", len(proposal), f"{share:.0f}% of the register")
    + metric("Max quality allowed", "0.40", "capped for proposal source")
    + metric("Claims raised", proposal["claim_id"].nunique(), "hypotheses, not facts")
    + "</div>"
)
```

## Claims that currently rest on the proposal

These are the items whose `source_type` is `proposal`. Each is a **hypothesis to be tested**, not a
fact — note the low `quality_score` and high `bias_risk`.

```{python}
#| label: proposal-table
#| echo: false
proposal[["evidence_id", "claim_id", "source_name", "date_collected",
          "quality_score", "bias_risk", "supports_claim", "notes"]]
```

## What would change a grade

A proposal claim moves off grade D only when **independent** evidence arrives — an interview, a
survey, a market report, or (decisively) an experiment/pilot.

This discipline now runs through the **evidence operating layer**: the current hypotheses are tracked
in `data/sample/claim_register.csv` (see [Claims & Confidence](claims-confidence.qmd)), and each is
raised toward grade B only when real discovery evidence — logged via the
[Evidence Register](evidence-register.qmd) — meets its stated bar. The proposal seeds the hypotheses;
it never validates them.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
This separation of *hypothesis source* from *evidence* is the reason the whole lab exists. The full
rationale is recorded in [Decision Record ADR-0002](../docs/decision-records/ADR-0002-cis-as-governance-model.md).
:::
